subject: [PATCH v5 3/6] fuse: add io-uring buffer pools

2026-06-30.patch_summary: This patch adds support for buffer pools to the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) io-uring implementation, allowing the server to pass a contiguous chunk of memory to the kernel for servicing requests. This decouples the buffer allocation from individual entries and enables the kernel to optimize memory usage by reusing buffers across multiple requests. The patch introduces a new command, FUSE_IO_URING_CMD_ADD_BUFPOOL, for registering a buffer pool per queue, and modifies the io-uring implementation to use a bitmap to track free buffers and report their byte offset to the server.
